diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "EssentialsTab" is 335 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 5 useState calls in "EssentialsTab" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state in `useReducer` so one logical update does not fan out into separate renders.

diagnostics.title: Autofocus on an element
diagnostics.message: `autoFocus` moves focus on load, which can disrupt screen reader and keyboard users. Remove it and let users choose where to focus.
diagnostics.help: Do not use `autoFocus`. It disorients users on load.

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "ActionItem" is 373 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Component rendered by inline function call
diagnostics.message: Your users lose state because "renderActionVariant()" builds UI from an inline call that React remounts, so pull it into its own component instead.
diagnostics.help: Make it a named component so React preserves its identity and does not remount its state.

diagnostics.title: Autofocus on an element
diagnostics.message: `autoFocus` moves focus on load, which can disrupt screen reader and keyboard users. Remove it and let users choose where to focus.
diagnostics.help: Do not use `autoFocus`. It disorients users on load.

diagnostics.title: Label missing associated control
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users can't tell which input this label names because it's tied to none, so add `htmlFor` or wrap the input inside it.
diagnostics.help: Tie every label to a control with `htmlFor`, or by nesting the input.

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "BulkActionMenu" is 313 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 5 useState calls in "BulkActionMenu" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state in `useReducer` so one logical update does not fan out into separate renders.

diagnostics.title: Static value rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `AREA_ORDER` inside `CategoriesCombobox` uses no local state but is rebuilt every render, so it looks new each time & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the value above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted and makes it look new every time.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
diagnostics.message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: Prefer explicit variant components
diagnostics.message: Component "PhaseCombobox" picks which component to render from 2 boolean props (isMulti, showText), which multiplies untestable variants. Split it into explicit variant components so each renders one clear path.
diagnostics.help: Replace boolean props that switch whole subtrees with explicit variant components, like `<ThreadComposer />` and `<EditMessageComposer />`, so each variant renders one clear path.

diagnostics.title: Component rendered by inline function call
diagnostics.message: Your users lose state because "renderProgressBar()" builds UI from an inline call that React remounts, so pull it into its own component instead.
diagnostics.help: Make it a named component so React preserves its identity and does not remount its state.

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<address>` than `role="group"`, so use `<address>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Click handler missing keyboard handler
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can't trigger this click handler because there's no keyboard one, so add `onKeyUp`, `onKeyDown`, or `onKeyPress`.
diagnostics.help: Pair `onClick` with a key handler so keyboard users can trigger it.

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<address>` than `role="group"`, so use `<address>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
diagnostics.message: forwardRef is dead weight in React 19, since ref is a normal prop now, so drop it & pass ref straight through.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers

diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "i".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
diagnostics.message: forwardRef is dead weight in React 19, since ref is a normal prop now, so drop it & pass ref straight through.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: Your cleanup may read the wrong node since the ref `destroyTimeoutRef.current` can change before it runs.

diagnostics.title: Data passed to parent via effect
diagnostics.message: Handing data back to a parent from a useEffect costs your users an extra render.
diagnostics.help: Fetch the data in the parent and pass it down as a prop (or return it from the hook), instead of handing it back up through a prop callback in a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#passing-data-to-the-parent

diagnostics.title: Transition duration too long
diagnostics.message: Your users wait through a sluggish 3000ms transition, so keep UI transitions under 1000ms & save longer ones for big page-load animations.
diagnostics.help: Keep UI transitions under 1s. Use about 100 to 150ms for instant feedback, 200 to 300ms for state changes, and 300 to 500ms for layout. Save longer ones for big page-load animations.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
diagnostics.message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: Ref initializer runs on every render
diagnostics.message: useRef(new Map()) rebuilds this value on every render & throws it away.
diagnostics.help: Initialize the ref lazily so expensive values are not rebuilt and discarded on every render.

diagnostics.title: Unstable context provider value
diagnostics.message: Every reader of this context redraws on each render because you build its `value` inline.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the context value in `useMemo` or move it outside the component so consumers do not redraw every render.

diagnostics.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
diagnostics.message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: .map().filter(Boolean) loops twice
diagnostics.message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter(Boolean) makes two passes, so use .flatMap() to change & drop items in one pass
diagnostics.help: Use `.flatMap(item => condition ? [value] : [])` to change and drop items in one pass, instead of building a throwaway array in between

diagnostics.title: Unstable context provider value
diagnostics.message: Every reader of this context redraws on each render because you build its `value` inline.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the context value in `useMemo` or move it outside the component so consumers do not redraw every render.

diagnostics.title: Unescaped JSON in HTML or script sink
diagnostics.message: JSON.stringify is embedded in HTML/script markup without HTML-escaping; data containing `</script>` or `<` breaks out and becomes XSS.
diagnostics.help: JSON.stringify does not HTML-escape, so a `</script>` (or `<`) in the data breaks out and becomes XSS. Use an HTML-safe serializer (serialize-javascript, devalue) or escape `<`, `>`, and `&`, or pass data via a JSON `<script type="application/json">` read with JSON.parse.

diagnostics.title: Unescaped JSON in HTML or script sink
diagnostics.message: JSON.stringify is embedded in HTML/script markup without HTML-escaping; data containing `</script>` or `<` breaks out and becomes XSS.
diagnostics.help: JSON.stringify does not HTML-escape, so a `</script>` (or `<`) in the data breaks out and becomes XSS. Use an HTML-safe serializer (serialize-javascript, devalue) or escape `<`, `>`, and `&`, or pass data via a JSON `<script type="application/json">` read with JSON.parse.

diagnostics.title: HTML injection sink with dynamic content
diagnostics.message: HTML is injected from a dynamic-looking source, which can become XSS if the value is user-controlled or unsanitized.
diagnostics.help: Prefer rendering structured React nodes. If HTML is required, sanitize with a well-reviewed sanitizer and keep the trust boundary close to the sink.

diagnostics.title: Unescaped JSON in HTML or script sink

diagnostics.help: Prefer rendering structured React nodes. If HTML is required, sanitize with a well-reviewed sanitizer and keep the trust boundary close to the sink.

diagnostics.message: Component "CreateAndEditAction" is 484 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 7 useState calls in "CreateAndEditAction" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state in `useReducer` so one logical update does not fan out into separate renders.

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `partners` is rebuilt every render, so `useMemo` runs every time.

diagnostics.title: State only used in handlers
diagnostics.message: Each update to "prevFetcherData" redraws your component for nothing because this useState is set but never shown on screen.
diagnostics.help: Use useRef instead of useState when the value is only set and never shown on screen. `ref.current = ...` updates it without redrawing the component.

diagnostics.title: .map().filter(Boolean) loops twice
diagnostics.message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter(Boolean) makes two passes, so use .flatMap() to change & drop items in one pass
diagnostics.help: Use `.flatMap(item => condition ? [value] : [])` to change and drop items in one pass, instead of building a throwaway array in between

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "HelpPage" is 557 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Static value rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `sections` inside `HelpPage` uses no local state but is rebuilt every render, so it looks new each time & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the value above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted and makes it look new every time.

diagnostics.title: Click handler missing keyboard handler
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can't trigger this click handler because there's no keyboard one, so add `onKeyUp`, `onKeyDown`, or `onKeyPress`.
diagnostics.help: Pair `onClick` with a key handler so keyboard users can trigger it.

diagnostics.title: Interaction on static element
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users can't tell this click handler is interactive because it has no `role`, so add a `role` or use a button or link.
diagnostics.help: Give clickable static elements a `role`, or use a button or link.

diagnostics.title: State initializer runs on every render
diagnostics.message: useState(parseISO()) re-runs parseISO() on every render & throws the result away.
diagnostics.help: Wrap expensive initial state in an arrow function so the initializer does not rerun and get thrown away on every render.

diagnostics.title: BaaS authority map shipped in browser artifact
diagnostics.message: A browser artifact exposes Firebase/Supabase config together with sensitive collections or authorization fields.
diagnostics.help: Client BaaS config is often public, but shipped collection names plus owner, role, tenant, or admin fields give attackers a precise authorization map. Verify rules/RLS enforce every boundary server-side.

diagnostics.message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter(Boolean) makes two passes, so use .flatMap() to change & drop items in one pass
diagnostics.help: Use `.flatMap(item => condition ? [value] : [])` to change and drop items in one pass, instead of building a throwaway array in between

diagnostics.title: Unstable context provider value
diagnostics.message: Every reader of this context redraws on each render because you build its `value` inline.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the context value in `useMemo` or move it outside the component so consumers do not redraw every render.

diagnostics.title: Unescaped JSON in HTML or script sink
diagnostics.message: JSON.stringify is embedded in HTML/script markup without HTML-escaping; data containing `</script>` or `<` breaks out and becomes XSS.
diagnostics.help: JSON.stringify does not HTML-escape, so a `</script>` (or `<`) in the data breaks out and becomes XSS. Use an HTML-safe serializer (serialize-javascript, devalue) or escape `<`, `>`, and `&`, or pass data via a JSON `<script type="application/json">` read with JSON.parse.

diagnostics.title: HTML injection sink with dynamic content
diagnostics.message: HTML is injected from a dynamic-looking source, which can become XSS if the value is user-controlled or unsanitized.
diagnostics.help: Prefer rendering structured React nodes. If HTML is required, sanitize with a well-reviewed sanitizer and keep the trust boundary close to the sink.

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change

diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 7 useState calls in "CreateAndEditAction" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state in `useReducer` so one logical update does not fan out into separate renders.

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `partners` is rebuilt every render, so `useMemo` runs every time.

diagnostics.title: State only used in handlers
diagnostics.message: Each update to "prevFetcherData" redraws your component for nothing because this useState is set but never shown on screen.
diagnostics.help: Use useRef instead of useState when the value is only set and never shown on screen. `ref.current = ...` updates it without redrawing the component.

diagnostics.title: Static value rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `sections` inside `HelpPage` uses no local state but is rebuilt every render, so it looks new each time & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the value above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted and makes it look new every time.

diagnostics.title: Click handler missing keyboard handler
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can't trigger this click handler because there's no keyboard one, so add `onKeyUp`, `onKeyDown`, or `onKeyPress`.
diagnostics.help: Pair `onClick` with a key handler so keyboard users can trigger it.

diagnostics.title: Interaction on static element
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users can't tell this click handler is interactive because it has no `role`, so add a `role` or use a button or link.
diagnostics.help: Give clickable static elements a `role`, or use a button or link.

diagnostics.title: State initializer runs on every render
diagnostics.message: useState(parseISO()) re-runs parseISO() on every render & throws the result away.
diagnostics.help: Wrap expensive initial state in an arrow function so the initializer does not rerun and get thrown away on every render.

diagnostics.title: BaaS authority map shipped in browser artifact
diagnostics.message: A browser artifact exposes Firebase/Supabase config together with sensitive collections or authorization fields.
diagnostics.help: Client BaaS config is often public, but shipped collection names plus owner, role, tenant, or admin fields give attackers a precise authorization map. Verify rules/RLS enforce every boundary server-side.
